an2.281-309:1.1:
"Bhikkhus, it is for these two reasons that the Tathāgata (281) has prescribed the Pāṭimokkha for his disciples …
…
(282) " … has prescribed the recitation of the Pāṭimokkha … "
(283) " … has prescribed the suspension of the Pāṭimokkha … "
(284) " … has prescribed the invitation ceremony … "
(285) " … has prescribed the suspension of the invitation ceremony … "
(286) " … has prescribed the legal act of censure … "
(287) " … has prescribed the legal act of
dependence … "
(288) " … has prescribed the legal act of banishment … "
(289) " … has prescribed the legal act of reconciliation … "
(290) " … has prescribed the legal act of suspension … "
(291) " … has prescribed the imposition of probation … "
(292) " … has prescribed sending back to the beginning … "
(293) " … has prescribed the imposition of penance … "
(294) " … has prescribed rehabilitation … "
(295) " … has prescribed reinstatement … "
(296) " … has prescribed removal … "
(297) " … has prescribed full ordination … "
(298) " … has prescribed a legal act that consists in a motion … "
(299) " … has prescribed a legal act that includes a motion as the second
… "
(300) " … has prescribed a legal act that includes a motion as the fourth
… "
(301) " … has prescribed
when none had been prescribed before … "
(302) " … has added an amendment
prescribed … "
(303) " … has prescribed removal by presence … "
(304) " … has prescribed removal on account of recollection … "
(305) " … has prescribed removal on account of
insanity … "
(306) " … has prescribed the acknowledgment
… "
(307) " … has prescribed
the majority … "
(308) " … has prescribed
aggravated misconduct … "
(309) " … has prescribed covering over with grass. What two? For the well-being of the Saṅgha and for the ease of the Saṅgha …
… For the continuation of the good Dhamma and for promoting the discipline. It is for these two reasons that the Tathāgata has prescribed the covering over with grass."
